Although tests of the proportional hazards assumption indicated evidence of non-proportionality for APOE4 status, time-varying coefficient models did not provide strong evidence of a time-dependent effect (p = 0.168 in the p-tau181 model and p = 0.189 in the NfL model). Therefore, the standard Cox proportional hazards model is retained for primary interpretation.

## For the multivariate joint model

### Survival object with global event, time_to_event and age_at_baseline

```{r}
surv_object_multi <- Surv(
  time = surv_data_multi$time_to_event, 
  event = surv_data_multi$event
  )
```

### Kaplan-Meier curve for global version of survival object

```{r}
# We fit Kaplan-Meier curve for global version of survival object
km_fit_multi <- survfit(surv_object_multi ~ 1, data = surv_data)
# Plot
plot(
  km_fit_multi, 
  xlab = "Days since p-tau181 baseline", 
  ylab = "Survival probability (no dementia)", 
  main = "Kaplan-Meier Curve for Dementia (global baseline)"
)
```

### Fitted Cox PH model with global survival object

```{r}
surv_model_multi <- coxph(
  surv_object_multi ~ age_at_baseline + PTGENDER + APOE4_status,
  data = surv_data_multi
)

summary(surv_model_multi)
```

### Test of the assumption of proportional hazards

```{r}
# For the Cox PH model with global survival object
cox.zph(surv_model_multi)
```

# Two separate joint models

## Fitted joint model for p-tau181

```{r}
library(JM)
# Joint model for p-tau181 using package JM (frequentist approach)
# joint_model_ptau <- jointModel(
#   long_model_ptau,   
#   surv_model_ptau,  
#   timeVar = "time_years_ptau"
# )
# 
# summary(joint_model_ptau)
```

```{r}
library(JMbayes2)
# Joint model for p-tau181 using package JMbayes2 (bayesian approach)
# joint_model_ptau <- jm(
#   surv_model_ptau,
#   long_model_ptau,
#   time_var = "time_years_ptau",
#   n_chains = 3L, 
#   n_iter = 11000L, 
#   n_burnin = 1000L
#   )
# 
# summary(joint_model_ptau)
```

Rhat-values a bit too high for time_years_ptau –\> Let's try with 20000 iterations instead:

```{r}
library(JMbayes2)
# Joint model for p-tau181 using package JMbayes2 (bayesian approach) - 20000 iterations
# joint_model_ptau <- jm(
#   surv_model_ptau,
#   long_model_ptau,
#   time_var = "time_years_ptau",
#   n_chains = 3L, 
#   n_iter = 20000L, 
#   n_burnin = 1000L
#   )
# 
# summary(joint_model_ptau)
```

## Fitted joint model for NfL

```{r}
# Joint model for NfL using package JM (frequentist approach)
# joint_model_nfl <- jointModel(
#   long_model_nfl,   
#   surv_model_nfl, 
#   timeVar = "time_years_nfl"
# )
# 
# summary(joint_model_nfl)
```

```{r}
# Joint model for NfL using package JMbayes2 (bayesian approach)
# joint_model_nfl <- jm(
#   surv_model_nfl,
#   long_model_nfl,
#   time_var = "time_years_nfl",
#   n_chains = 3L, 
#   n_iter = 11000L, 
#   n_burnin = 1000L
#   )
# 
# summary(joint_model_nfl)
```

### Traceplots

```{r}
# traceplot(joint_model_ptau)
# traceplot(joint_model_nfl)
```

```{r}
# traceplot(joint_model_ptau, parm = "betas")
# traceplot(joint_model_ptau, parm = "alphas")
# traceplot(joint_model_ptau, parm = "sigmas")
```

### Density plots

```{r}
# densplot(joint_model_ptau)
# densplot(joint_model_nfl)
```

```{r}
# densplot(joint_model_ptau, parm = "betas")
# densplot(joint_model_ptau, parm = "alphas")
# densplot(joint_model_ptau, parm = "sigmas")
```

# Multivariate joint model

## To check if the models have stable random slopes

```{r}
summary(long_model_ptau)
VarCorr(long_model_nfl)
```

For both models, the slope variance is very low (lme: 0.0011, mixed_model: 0.036), which indicates that individual biomarker trajectories over time show minimal variation --\> We skip the random slope in fForms:

## fForms

```{r}
fForms <- list(
  "log_PLASMAPTAU181" = ~ value(log_PLASMAPTAU181), 
  "log_PLASMA_NFL" = ~ value(log_PLASMA_NFL) 
  )      
```

## Fitted multivariate joint model

```{r}
# multi_joint_model <- jm(surv_model_multi, mixed ,
#                         time_var = "time_years",
#                         functional_forms = fForms,
#                         n_chains = 3L,    
#                         n_iter = 20000L,
#                         n_burnin = 2000L
#                         )
# 
# summary(multi_joint_model)
```

```{r}
# summary <- summary(multi_joint_model)
# summary$Survival
```

# ...

# Latent Class Mixed Models

# LCMM: NFL

Latent class mixed models for NFL

-   First using the NFL specific data set

## M0 (1 latent class)

```{r}
hlme_m0 <- hlme(
  fixed = log_PLASMA_NFL ~ time_years_nfl,
  random = ~ time_years_nfl,
  subject = "RID",
  data = long_data_nfl
)
```

```{r}
summary(hlme_m0)
```

## M2 (2 latent classes)

```{r}
hlme_m1 <- hlme(
    fixed = log_PLASMA_NFL ~ time_years_nfl,
    mixture = ~ time_years_nfl,
    random = ~ time_years_nfl,
    subject = "RID",
    classmb = ~ 1,
    ng = 2,
    data = long_data_nfl,
    B = random(hlme_m0)
  )

summary(hlme_m1)
```

### Posterior probabilities

```{r}
postprob(hlme_m1)
```

## Visualisation

### Distribution of posterior probabilities (class 1)

```{r}
# saving the posterior probabilities into a data frame
pp <- hlme_m1$pprob
```

```{r}
# plotting posterior probabilities for class 1
ggplot(pp, aes(x = prob1)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, colour = "white", fill = "cornflowerblue") +
  labs(title = "Posterior Probabilities for Class 1") +
  theme_classic()
```

### Distribution of posterior probabilities (class 2)

```{r}
# plotting posterior probabilities for class 2
ggplot(pp, aes(x = prob2)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, colour = "white", fill = "cornflowerblue") +
  labs(title = "Posterior Probabilities for Class 1") +
  theme_classic()
```

### Class certainty scatterplot

```{r}
# scatterplot of class certainty
ggplot(pp, aes(prob1, prob2)) +
  geom_point(alpha = .8) +
  geom_abline(slope = -1, intercept = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(x = "P(Class 1)", y = "P(Class 2)") +
  theme_classic()
```

## Comparing M0 and M1

```{r}
summarytable(hlme_m0, hlme_m1, which = c("G", "loglik", "BIC", "entropy"))
```

# LCMM: PTAU

Latent class mixed models for PTAU

## M0 (1 latent class)

```{r}
hlme_m0 <- hlme(
  fixed = log_PLASMAPTAU181 ~ time_years_ptau,
  random = ~ time_years_ptau,
  subject = "RID",
  data = long_data_ptau
)
```

```{r}
summary(hlme_m0)
```

## M2 (2 latent classes)

```{r}
hlme_m1 <- hlme(
    fixed = log_PLASMAPTAU181 ~ time_years_ptau,
    mixture = ~ time_years_ptau,
    random = ~ time_years_ptau,
    subject = "RID",
    classmb = ~ 1,
    ng = 2,
    data = long_data_ptau,
    B = random(hlme_m0)
  )
```

```{r}
summary(hlme_m1)
```

### Posterior probabilities

```{r}
postprob(hlme_m1)
```

## Visualisation

```{r}
# saving the posterior probabilities into a data frame
pp <- hlme_m1$pprob
```

### Distribution of posterior probabilities (class 1)

```{r}
# plotting posterior probabilities for class 1
ggplot(pp, aes(x = prob1)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, colour = "white", fill = "cornflowerblue") +
  labs(title = "Posterior Probabilities for Class 1") +
  theme_classic()
```

### Distribution of posterior probabilities (class 2)

```{r}
# plotting posterior probabilities for class 2
ggplot(pp, aes(x = prob2)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, colour = "white", fill = "cornflowerblue") +
  labs(title = "Posterior Probabilities for Class 1") +
  theme_classic()
```

### Class certainty scatterplot

```{r}
# scatterplot of class certainty
ggplot(pp, aes(prob1, prob2)) +
  geom_point(alpha = .8) +
  geom_abline(slope = -1, intercept = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(x = "P(Class 1)", y = "P(Class 2)") +
  theme_classic()
```

## Comparing M0 and M1

```{r}
summarytable(hlme_m0, hlme_m1, which = c("G", "loglik", "BIC", "entropy"))
```

# LCMM: NFL (including classmb covariates)

Latent class mixed models for NFL including covariates in the class model

## M0 (1 latent class)

```{r}
# updating the 1 class model
hlme_m0 <- hlme(
  fixed = log_PLASMA_NFL ~ time_years_nfl,
  random = ~ time_years_nfl,
  subject = "RID",
  data = long_data_nfl
)
```

## M2 (2 latent classes)

```{r}
hlme_m1 <- hlme(
    fixed = log_PLASMA_NFL ~ time_years_nfl,
    mixture = ~ time_years_nfl,
    random = ~ time_years_nfl,
    subject = "RID",
    classmb = ~ APOE4_status + age_at_visit + PTGENDER,
    ng = 2,
    data = long_data_nfl,
    B = random(hlme_m0)
  )
```

```{r}
summary(hlme_m1)
```

### Posterior probabilities

```{r}
postprob(hlme_m1)
```

## Visualisation

```{r}
# saving the posterior probabilities into a data frame
pp <- hlme_m1$pprob
```

### Distribution of posterior probabilities (class 1)

```{r}
# plotting posterior probabilities for class 1
ggplot(pp, aes(x = prob1)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, colour = "white", fill = "cornflowerblue") +
  labs(title = "Posterior Probabilities for Class 1") +
  theme_classic()
```

### Distribution of posterior probabilities (class 2)

```{r}
# plotting posterior probabilities for class 2
ggplot(pp, aes(x = prob2)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, colour = "white", fill = "cornflowerblue") +
  labs(title = "Posterior Probabilities for Class 1") +
  theme_classic()
```

### Class certainty scatterplot

```{r}
# scatterplot of class certainty
ggplot(pp, aes(prob1, prob2)) +
  geom_point(alpha = .8) +
  geom_abline(slope = -1, intercept = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(x = "P(Class 1)", y = "P(Class 2)") +
  theme_classic()
```

## Comparing M0 and M1

```{r}
summarytable(hlme_m0, hlme_m1, which = c("G", "loglik", "BIC", "entropy"))
```

# LCMM: PTAU (including classmb covariates)

Latent class mixed models for PTAU including covariates in the class model

## M0 (1 latent class)

```{r}
# updating the 1 class model
hlme_m0 <- hlme(
  fixed = log_PLASMAPTAU181 ~ time_years_ptau,
  random = ~ time_years_ptau,
  subject = "RID",
  data = long_data_ptau
)
```

## M2 (2 latent classes)

```{r}
hlme_m1 <- hlme(
    fixed = log_PLASMAPTAU181 ~ time_years_ptau,
    mixture = ~ time_years_ptau,
    random = ~ time_years_ptau,
    subject = "RID",
    classmb = ~ APOE4_status + age_at_visit + PTGENDER,
    ng = 2,
    data = long_data_ptau,
    B = random(hlme_m0)
  )
```

```{r}
summary(hlme_m1)
```

### Posterior probabilities

```{r}
postprob(hlme_m1)
```

## Visualisation

```{r}
# saving the posterior probabilities into a data frame
pp <- hlme_m1$pprob
```

### Distribution of posterior probabilities (class 1)

```{r}
# plotting posterior probabilities for class 1
ggplot(pp, aes(x = prob1)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, colour = "white", fill = "cornflowerblue") +
  labs(title = "Posterior Probabilities for Class 1") +
  theme_classic()
```

### Distribution of posterior probabilities (class 2)

```{r}
# plotting posterior probabilities for class 1
ggplot(pp, aes(x = prob2)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, colour = "white", fill = "cornflowerblue") +
  labs(title = "Posterior Probabilities for Class 2") +
  theme_classic()
```

### Class certainty scatterplot

```{r}
# scatterplot of class certainty
ggplot(pp, aes(prob1, prob2)) +
  geom_point(alpha = .8) +
  geom_abline(slope = -1, intercept = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(x = "P(Class 1)", y = "P(Class 2)") +
  theme_classic()
```

## Comparing M0 and M1

```{r}
summarytable(hlme_m0, hlme_m1, which = c("G", "loglik", "BIC", "entropy"))
```

# MLCMM: NFL & PTAU

Multivariate latent class mixed models with both NFL and PTAU

## M0 (1 latent class)

```{r}
mlcmm_m0 <- multlcmm(
  fixed = log_PLASMA_NFL + log_PLASMAPTAU181 ~ time_years,
  random = ~ time_years,
  subject = "RID",
  data = long_data_multi
)
```

## M2 (2 latent classes)

```{r}
mlcmm_m1 <- multlcmm(
  fixed = log_PLASMA_NFL + log_PLASMAPTAU181 ~ time_years,
  random = ~ time_years,
  mixture = ~ time_years,
  subject = "RID",
  classmb = ~ 1,
  ng = 2,
  data = long_data_multi,
  B = random(mlcmm_m0)
)
```

```{r}
summary(mlcmm_m1)
```

### Posterior probabilities

```{r}
postprob(mlcmm_m1)
```

## M2 (2 latent classes) (including class covariates)

```{r}
mlcmm_m1 <- multlcmm(
  fixed = log_PLASMA_NFL + log_PLASMAPTAU181 ~ time_years,
  random = ~ time_years,
  mixture = ~ time_years,
  subject = "RID",
  classmb = ~ APOE4_status + age_at_visit + PTGENDER,
  ng = 2,
  data = long_data_multi,
  B = random(mlcmm_m0)
)
```

```{r}
summary(mlcmm_m1)
```

### Posterior probabilities

```{r}
postprob(mlcmm_m1)
```

## Visualisation

```{r}
# saving the posterior probabilities into a data frame
pp <- mlcmm_m1$pprob
```

### Distribution of posterior probabilities (class 1)

```{r}
# plotting posterior probabilities for class 1
ggplot(pp, aes(x = prob1)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, colour = "white", fill = "cornflowerblue") +
  labs(title = "Posterior Probabilities for Class 1") +
  theme_classic()
```

### Distribution of posterior probabilities (class 2)

```{r}
# plotting posterior probabilities for class 2
ggplot(pp, aes(x = prob2)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, colour = "white", fill = "cornflowerblue") +
  labs(title = "Posterior Probabilities for Class 2") +
  theme_classic()
```

### Class certainty scatterplot

```{r}
# scatterplot of class certainty
ggplot(pp, aes(prob1, prob2)) +
  geom_point(alpha = .8) +
  geom_abline(slope = -1, intercept = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(x = "P(Class 1)", y = "P(Class 2)") +
  theme_classic()
```

## Comparing M0 and M1

```{r}
summarytable(mlcmm_m0, mlcmm_m1, which = c("G", "loglik", "BIC", "entropy"))
```

# JLCMM: NFL

1.  Posterior probabilities show good classification

2.  Score test statistic for conditional independence is significant with 1 class and not significant with 2 classes: evidence for 2 classes

3.  No covariates in the survival model OR the class membership model, evidence for latent classes?

```{r}
# merging the longitudinal and survival data for NfL
jlcmm_data_nfl <- surv_data_nfl %>%
select(RID, event_nfl, time_to_event_nfl) %>%
  left_join(long_data_nfl, by = "RID")
```

## M0 (1 latent class)

```{r}
jlcmm_m0 <- jlcmm(
  fixed = log_PLASMA_NFL ~ time_years_nfl,
  random = ~ 1 + time_years_nfl,
  subject = "RID",
  survival = Surv(time_to_event_nfl, event_nfl) ~ 1,
  data = jlcmm_data_nfl
)
```

```{r}
summary(jlcmm_m0)
```

## M1 (2 latent classes)

```{r}
jlcmm_m1 <- jlcmm(
  fixed = log_PLASMA_NFL ~ time_years_nfl,
  random = ~ 1 + time_years_nfl,
  mixture = ~ time_years_nfl,
  classmb = ~ 1,
  ng = 2,
  subject = "RID",
  survival = Surv(time_to_event_nfl, event_nfl) ~ 1,
  data = jlcmm_data_nfl,
  B = random(jlcmm_m0)
)
```

```{r}
summary(jlcmm_m1)
```

### Posterior probabilities

```{r}
postprob(jlcmm_m1)
```

## Visualisation

1.  Need to check probYT1 and probYT2

```{r}
# saving the posterior probabilities into a data frame
# pp <- jlcmm_m1$pprob
# pp1 <- pp %>% filter(class == 1)
# pp2 <- pp %>% filter(class == 2)
```

### Distribution of posterior probabilities (class 1)

1.  Check that YT1 is for the joint model, not the longitudinal model only

```{r}
# plotting posterior probabilities for class 1
# ggplot(pp1, aes(x = probYT1)) + 
#   geom_histogram(bins = 30, colour = "white", fill = "cornflowerblue") +
#   labs(title = "Posterior Probabilities for Class 1") +
#   theme_classic()
```

### Distribution of posterior probabilities (class 2)

```{r}
# plotting posterior probabilities for class 2
# ggplot(pp2, aes(x = probYT1)) + 
#   geom_histogram(bins = 30, colour = "white", fill = "cornflowerblue") +
#   labs(title = "Posterior Probabilities for Class 2") +
#   theme_classic()
```

### Class certainty scatterplot

```{r}
# scatterplot of class certainty
# ggplot(pp, aes(prob1, prob2)) +
#   geom_point(alpha = .8) +
#   geom_abline(slope = -1, intercept = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
#   labs(x = "P(Class 1)", y = "P(Class 2)") +
#   theme_classic()
```

## Comparing M0 and M1

```{r}
#summarytable(jlcmm_m0, jlcmm_m1, which = c("G", "loglik", "BIC", "entropy"))
```

# JLCMM: PTAU

```{r}
# merging the longitudinal and survival data for PTAU
jlcmm_data_ptau <- surv_data_ptau %>%
  select(RID, event_ptau, time_to_event_ptau) %>%
  merge(long_data_ptau, by = "RID")
```

## M0 (1 latent class)

```{r}
jlcmm_m0 <- jlcmm(
  fixed = log_PLASMAPTAU181 ~ time_years_ptau,
  random = ~ 1 + time_years_ptau,
  subject = "RID",
  survival = Surv(time_to_event_ptau, event_ptau) ~ 1,
  data = jlcmm_data_ptau
)
```

```{r}
summary(jlcmm_m0)
```

## M1 (2 latent classes)

```{r}
jlcmm_m1 <- jlcmm(
  fixed = log_PLASMAPTAU181 ~ time_years_ptau,
  random = ~ 1 + time_years_ptau,
  mixture = ~ time_years_ptau,
  classmb = ~ 1,
  ng = 2,
  subject = "RID",
  survival = Surv(time_to_event_ptau, event_ptau) ~ 1,
  data = jlcmm_data_ptau,
  B = random(jlcmm_m0)
)
```

```{r}
summary(jlcmm_m1)
```

### Posterior probabilities

```{r}
postprob(jlcmm_m1)
```

## Visualisation

### Distribution of posterior probabilities (class 1)

```{r}

```

### Distribution of posterior probabilities (class 2)

```{r}

```

### Class certainty scatterplot

```{r}

```

## Comparing M0 and M1

```{r}
summarytable(jlcmm_m0, jlcmm_m1, which = c("G", "loglik", "BIC", "entropy"))
```

## M2 (3 latent classes)

*Things to assess:*

1.  *Fit 4 class model -\> does BIC improve any more or plateau?*

2.  *Score test now not significant compared with the two class model*

3.  *VERY small class 1*

4.  *CLass 2 and 3 are not cleanly separated (look at posterior probabilities)*

5.  *Plot survival curves (how?)*

### gridsearch()

```{r}
# gridsearch(m = jlcmm(
#   fixed = log_PLASMAPTAU181 ~ time_years_ptau,
#   random = ~ 1 + time_years_ptau,
#   mixture = ~ time_years_ptau,
#   classmb = ~ 1,
#   ng = 2,
#   subject = "RID",
#   survival = Surv(time_to_event_ptau, event_ptau) ~ 1,
#   data = jlcmm_data_ptau,
#   B = random(jlcmm_m0)),
#   minit = jlcmm_m1,
#   rep = 50,
#   maxiter = 30)

```

```{r}
#jlcmm_m2 <- jlcmm(
  #fixed = log_PLASMAPTAU181 ~ time_years_ptau,
  #random = ~ 1 + time_years_ptau,
  #mixture = ~ time_years_ptau,
  #classmb = ~ 1,
  #ng = 3,
  #subject = "RID",
  #survival = Surv(time_to_event_ptau, event_ptau) ~ 1,
  #data = jlcmm_data_ptau,
  #B = random(jlcmm_m0)
#)
```

```{r}
#summary(jlcmm_m2)
```

```{r}
#postprob(jlcmm_m2)
```

## Comparing M0, M1, M3

```{r}
summarytable(jlcmm_m0, jlcmm_m1, jlcmm_m2,
             which = c("G", "loglik", "BIC", "entropy"))
```

```{r}
# saving which class each individual was predicted to be in
class_preds <- jlcmm_m2$pprobY

# merging with genotype data
class_preds <- class_preds %>%
  merge(apoe %>% select(RID, APOE4_status), by = "RID")

# checking the distribution of genotype by class
table(class_preds$class, class_preds$APOE4_status)
```

# MJLCMM: NFL & PTAU

```{r}
# merging the longitudinal and survival data for NfL
jlcmm_data_multi <- surv_data_multi %>%
  select(RID, event, time_to_event) %>%
  merge(long_data_multi, by = "RID")
```

```{r}
# fitting multivariate latent class model with 1 class and 2 classes with jlcmm_data_multi

mlcmm_m0 <- multlcmm(
  fixed = log_PLASMA_NFL + log_PLASMAPTAU181 ~ time_years,
  random = ~ time_years,
  subject = "RID",
  data = jlcmm_data_multi
)

mlcmm_m1 <- multlcmm(
  fixed = log_PLASMA_NFL + log_PLASMAPTAU181 ~ time_years,
  random = ~ time_years,
  mixture = ~ time_years,
  subject = "RID",
  classmb = ~ 1,
  ng = 2,
  data = jlcmm_data_multi,
  B = random(mlcmm_m0)
)
```

## M0 (1 latent class)

```{r}
mult_jlcmm_m0 <- mpjlcmm(
  longitudinal = list(mlcmm_m0),
  subject = "RID",
  survival = Surv(time_to_event, event) ~ 1,
  ng = 1,
  data = jlcmm_data_multi
)
```

```{r}
summary(mult_jlcmm_m0)
```

## M1 (2 latent classes)

```{r}
mult_jlcmm_m1 <- mpjlcmm(
  longitudinal = list(mlcmm_m1),
  subject = "RID",
  survival = Surv(time_to_event, event) ~ 1,
  ng = 2,
  data = jlcmm_data_multi,
  B = random(mult_jlcmm_m0)
)
```

```{r}
summary(mult_jlcmm_m1)
```

### Posterior probabilities

```{r}
postprob(mult_jlcmm_m1)
```

## Visualisation

### Distribution of posterior probabilities (class 1)

```{r}

```

### Distribution of posterior probabilities (class 2)

```{r}

```

### Class certainty scatterplot

```{r}

```

## Comparing M0 and M1

```{r}
summarytable(mult_jlcmm_m0, mult_jlcmm_m1, which = c("G", "loglik", "BIC", "entropy"))
```

## Comparing models
